highlights_headline: This unreleased build brings significant P/Invoke hot-path optimisations and upstream Skia m151 bug fixes.
highlights_body: Four allocation-free interop improvements cut per-call heap pressure in HarfBuzzSharp and SKShaper, while two upstream Skia sync merges carry engine-level bug fixes from Chrome m151.

bullets.lead: Upstream Skia m151 bug fixes merged
bullets.detail: Two rounds of upstream Chrome/m151 patches have been pulled in, bringing rendering correctness and stability improvements from the Skia project.

bullets.lead: SKColor hex parsing optimised with ReadOnlySpan overloads
bullets.detail: SKColor.Parse and TryParse now accept ReadOnlySpan<char>, avoiding string allocations on hot paths and improving throughput for colour-lookup-heavy workloads.
The unnecessary unsafe keyword on GRContextOptions has been dropped; consuming code no longer needs to allow unsafe blocks to reference this type.

bullets.lead: HarfBuzzSharp and SKShaper interop made allocation-free
bullets.detail: Buffer.AddUtf8(string) now uses a pooled UTF-8 encode instead of allocating, Tag.Parse and SKFourByteTag.Parse drop their per-call char[4] allocations, and SKShaper glyph results are read via zero-copy spans — reducing GC pressure on text-shaping hot paths.

contributor_summaries.ramezgerges: Added a cross-backend golden-image visual regression test harness to the test suite
